dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for reported diabetic issue at the intersection.
Hi, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I've been having elevated blood sugar, and I'm feeling weak with around a 7 out of 10 stomach pain.
Pt is alert and oriented x4-person, place, time, and event (AO×4).
Strong and regular bilateral radial pulses. Pt has a patent airway and can maintain it independently.
First set: 92 pulse, 12 breaths per minute, blood pressure 142 over 77. Blood glucose is 430.
SpO2 is at 100%, and EKG is showing sinus rhythm.
Second round: BP of 88 over 49, pulse 79, resp rate 12. Blood glucose is zero. SpO2 at 99%.
Third reading: BP 118 over 76, pulse 77, respiratory rate 14, and SpO2 is back to 100%.
Warm, dry, intact skin with appropriate color. No pallor noted.
No jvd or td detected. Pupils are sluggish at 4 mm.
Abdomen is soft, non-tender, and non-distended with palpation. Equal chest rise on both sides.
Pt states she's been in and out of the hospital for hyperglycemia with no long-term resolution. Starts about 12 hours ago.
She's taking Lantus, NovoLog, gabapentin, trazodone, and Depakote.
Allergies include shellfish and Erythromycin (E.E.S.).
History includes insulin-dependent diabetes, neuropathy w/diabetes, PTSD, and bipolar disorder.
Pt is currently presenting as mildly lethargic with decreased reflex response.
Upper and lower extremities are intact with no trauma. Peripheral pulses are present.
Placed on a cardiac monitor with 4-lead ECG.
Initiated IV in left arm with saline lock and administered 100 ml of normal saline.
Tympanic temperature obtained and reads 98.1°F.
Managing under Medical - Diabetic - Hyperglycemia (protocol 3-7).
Pt was transferred into a semi-Fowler's position on the stretcher and secured for transport.
Transporting non-emergent to [COMPANY_NAME] per pt request and as the closest appropriate facility.
Pt's condition remains stable during transport. No changes noted.
Report handed off to [COMPANY_NAME] without incident using the draw-sheet lateral slide method.
Retake vitals showing stable but fluctuating BP and glucose readings.
Pt took insulin yesterday and this morning due to high blood sugar.
Pt is compliant with medications, with no recent changes or missed doses.
Pt also reports a history of epilepsy (seizures) and mental health conditions.
No drainage or obstruction noted in nose or ears. Appearance of mouth is normal.
Normal breath sounds bilaterally.
No deformities or loss of function in any extremities.
Pt last consumed food yesterday evening, prior to measuring high blood sugar.
Pt uses fast-acting insulin when blood sugar is elevated.
EKG remains sinus rhythm throughout the encounter.
Describes abdominal pain as cramping, intermittent, and constant.
Rated the pain as a 7 on a 10-point scale.
Pain started approximately within the hour.
No additional medications administered per protocol and physician orders.
Capillary blood glucose tested and found to be abnormal at 430 and 0.
Skin integrity is preserved with no signs of infection or bleeding.
No signs of peritonitis or acute distress reported by the patient.
Pt's Neuropathy w/Diabetes may be affecting sensation in extremities.
Pt denies current bowel sounds and has not disclosed recent bowel movements.
No neurological deficits detected in upper or lower extremities.
Pt maintains alert and oriented x4 status throughout the assessment.
Pt does not experience any pelvic pain or instability.
O2 saturation remains above 95% with room air, no supplemental oxygen needed.
No neck masses, lymphadenopathy, or signs of trauma.
No skin turgor or edema. Temperature is normal.
IV fluid administered at a maintenance rate.
Pulse was normal in the first two assessments, and slowed on the third.
Pt has a documented history of endocrine diabetes with complications.
Pt was alert and oriented x4 at time of presentation and examination.
Pt's Neuropathy w/Diabetes may be affecting sensation in extremities.
No rashes or bite marks on the skin during exam.
No auscultation of adventitious breath or heart sounds.
Pt responds to verbal commands appropriately and fully.
Swallowing is not reported to be impaired by pt.
Visual inspection shows abdomen to be flat, with no visible distention.
Pt denies any recent sick contacts or travel prior to calling.
Pt reported no new symptoms other than hyperglycemia and distress.
Blood glucose could not be obtained in the second and third readings.
Skin turgor is within normal limits, eyes moist and skin elastic.
Oxygen levels remain stable without any changes or intervention.
Pt has no sunken eyes or signs of dehydration.
No discomfort reported on palpation of abdomen.
Pt has a history of epilepsy (seizures) and related concerns.
No evidence of previous scars related to diabetes management.
Pt has not experienced vomiting or diarrhea recently.
EKG has remained sinus rhythm with no additional dysrhythmias.
No signs of hives or allergic reactions outlined in notes.
Pt reports no drug allergies aside from previously stated shellfish and Erythromycin.
No evidence of obstruction or foreign body in nasal or oral cavity.
Blood pressure fluctuated but remains within safe limits.
Pt's posture was observed to be in LR Recumbent, as reported on scene.
Bowel sounds showed no abnormalities during palpation.
Rechecked and recognized a trend of decreasing status of glucose.
Heart rate decreased over time but remained within normal range.
Low BP in second reading was noted, but returned in the third to a more acceptable range.
No cardiac or neurological concerns during ongoing assessment.
Pt moved well into the stretcher with full cooperation.
Oxygen saturation levels improved in the third reading from 99 to 100%.
No rash, hives, or skin irritations observed during skin assessment.
Continuous cardiac monitoring was employed and no changes occurred.
The source and possible cause of low second and third glucose readings unknown.
Pt's memory and cognitive function seem intact with no obvious deficits.
Pt's skin remained consistent in appearance, with no macules, papules, or vesicles.
Pt called 911 after insulin use and further glucose check showed no improvement.
Pt's movement appeared uncoordinated due to slight neurological presentation and weakness.
Pt had normal appearance of eyes and hearing, intact cognition, and no focal neurological deficits.
Pt is alert and oriented to person, place, time and event with a GCS of 15.
